purpose: Adds Playwright e2e coverage for the nameless-invitee onboarding path (gate blocks -> name entered -> admin sees the name -> admin approves -> invitee lands in the group with no second click) and preserves/extends coverage of the existing named-user path. No production module is created or owned by this story -- it consumes S1-S4's already-shipped production surface (JoinRequestCard, PendingRequestsSection, joinRequestSender/Handler, welcomeSubscription auto-accept) purely through the app's own UI and public helpers. A cross-epic documentation amendment (AC-8 in specs/epic-group-invite-links/) is bundled per stories.json's note that the amendment has no AC id of its own in this epic.
dependencies_allowed: Production data-testid surface already shipped by S1-S4: join-request-card, join-request-name-gate, join-request-name-input, join-request-send-btn, join-request-sent, pending-requests-section, pending-request-row-*, approve-request-*, pending-invitations-section, accept-invitation-*, group-card-*, group-detail-page, notification-badge, groups-empty-state, groups-list
dependencies_forbidden: Any change to app/src/** (S1-S4 production files are frozen for this story)
Raw WebSocket publish to strfry from test code (AC-ETE-3) -- all peer actions go through a second browser.newContext() driving the app's own UI/helpers
test_targets: app/tests/e2e/groups-invite-link.spec.ts::Invite link flow — generate, join request, approve (existing named-user describe block, 6 tests, extended with a no-gate assertion and an auto-accept-aware final assertion)
app/tests/e2e/groups-invite-link.spec.ts::Invite link flow — nameless invitee gate, nickname transport, auto-accept (new describe block, 5 tests, AC-ETE-1)
notes: This story creates no new seam and consumes no seam annotated cross_story in stories.json (the only epic seam, AuthenticatedUnwrapResult, is S3->S4 internal). The AC-8 doc amendment touches a DIFFERENT epic's spec file (specs/epic-group-invite-links/acceptance-criteria.md), explicitly permitted by this epic's spec.md #Relationship-to-Other-Epics and stories.json S5 scope -- it is documentation, not a module, and is listed here for completeness rather than as an owned module.
